# CWE-1052 positive: hard-coded database DSN literal (pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-1052-vulnerable.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

// CWE-1052: Excessive Use of Hard-Coded Literals in Initialization.
// Vulnerable: Open called with embedded username, password, and host strings.

import (
	"database/sql"
	"net/http"
)

const appDSNPure = "host=db.internal user=app_admin password=SuperSecret99 dbname=orders sslmode=disable"

func OpenAppDBPure() (*sql.DB, error) {
	return sql.Open("postgres", appDSNPure)
}

func HealthWithDBPure() http.HandlerFunc {
	db, _ := OpenAppDBPure()
	return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		if db == nil {
			http.Error(w, `{"db":"down"}`, http.StatusServiceUnavailable)
			return
		}
		if err := db.Ping(); err != nil {
			http.Error(w, `{"db":"down"}`, http.StatusServiceUnavailable)
			return
		}
		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
		w.Write([]byte(`{"db":"up"}`))
	}
}
